summary: The session focused on meaning-for-meaning transcription services, specifically TypeWell and C-Print, which provide real-time transcriptions for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing. The discussion highlighted the methods and applications of these transcription services in various settings, particularly in education.
facts: TypeWell is a meaning-for-meaning transcription service that uses trained transcribers to provide real-time transcriptions of lectures and meetings. C-Print is another meaning-for-meaning transcription service that employs trained transcribers to deliver real-time transcriptions using a phonetic-based abbreviation system. Both TypeWell and C-Print services are commonly used in educational settings to provide communication access for deaf or hard of hearing students.
